title: cordyline inscripta
intro: Cordyline inscripta, also known as the cabbage tree or kiokio, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its broad, flat leaves that resemble a cabbage. The leaves and roots of Cordyline inscripta have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Maori culture, for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding digestion, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, Cordyline inscripta is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to treat a range of conditions, from respiratory issues to skin irritations.
